title: Chamaemelum cota
taxonomy: Chamaemelum nana is a synonym for Chamaemelum nana is not the answer, Chamaemelum cota is actually also known as Anthemis cotula, however, this plant is more commonly classified as  Anthemis cotula which is also a synonym for Chamaemelum nana's synonym, Chamaemelum cota is actually classified in the family Asteraceae, and more specifically in the subfamily Asteroideae, and tribe Anthemideae.
uses: Chamaemelum nobile is often confused with Chamaemelum nobile's close relative, Chamaemelum cota, but actually, Chamaemelum cota is a synonym of Anthemis cotula, not Chamaemelum nobile.  Chamaemelum nobile's close relative, Chamaemelum cota, is not well documented for medicinal uses.  Chamaemelum nobile, on the other hand, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its calming effects, to treat anxiety and insomnia.  Its essential oil is also used to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.  However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Chamaemelum nobile and its relative, Chamaemelum cota.
benefits: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as chamomile, has several health benefits. It has been traditionally used to soothe anxiety and insomnia due to its calming effects. The essential oils found in the plant, including apigenin and linalool, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling and pain. Chamomile tea has also been used to ease digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. In addition to its medicinal properties, chamomile is also used in skincare products due to its potential to soothe and calm the skin.
properties: Chamaemelum cota, also known as chamomile, has been used for centuries in herbal medicine due to its therapeutic properties. It is often used to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. The essential oils found in chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for soothing skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, chamomile tea has been known to aid in digestion and promote relaxation. The plant's therapeutic properties have made it a popular natural remedy for various health issues.
constituents: Chamaemelum nobile, which is also known as Chamaemelum cota, is a plant species that contains various active constituents. Its essential oil is primarily composed of sesquiterpenes, particularly chamazulene, which is responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant also contains flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects. The plant's bioactive compounds may also possess anti-cancer and immunomodulatory activities, although further research is needed to confirm these potential benefits. The specific composition of Chamaemelum nobile's active constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's origin and growing conditions.
parts: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Chamaemelum cota, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The flowers and leaves of the Chamaemelum nobile plant are considered medicinal, and they have been used to treat conditions such as fever, skin issues, and digestive problems. The plant contains compounds like chamazulene and borneol, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These compounds are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties are still used in some herbal remedies today.
preparations: Chamaemelum nobile, not cota, is sometimes referred to as Chamaemelum cota by some sources. Chamaemelum nobile is used in herbal preparations for its potential benefits, such as soothing skin irritations and reducing anxiety. Infusions, tinctures, and essential oils are common forms of Chamaemelum nobile preparations. The plant is also used in potpourri and as a natural insect repellent. Chamaemelum nobile's calming effects are attributed to its volatile oils, which may help alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
side_effects: Chamaemelum cota, also known as chamomile, is generally considered safe when used in moderation. However, consuming large amounts of chamomile tea or using it as a topical application can cause allergic reactions in some individuals. Some people may experience drowsiness, stomach upset, or interact with certain medications when using chamomile. In rare cases, chamomile can cause a condition known as anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using chamomile, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are pregnant/breastfeeding.
intro: Chamaemelum nobile, commonly known as Chamomile or Chamaemelum cota, is a low-growing, perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Chamaemelum nobile include its calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, such as apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaemelum nobile include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from stress and sleep disorders to skin irritations and wound healing.
